content:
From a flow-net diagram drawn under a concrete dam, following information are obtained:

(i) The head difference between upstream and downstream side of the dam is 9 m .

(ii) The total number of equipotential drops between upstream and downstream side of the dam is 10 .

(iii) The length of the field nearest to the toe of the dam in the downstream side is 1 m .

If the soil below the dam is having a saturated unit weight of $21 \mathrm{kN} / \mathrm{m}^3$ and unit weight of water is $9.81 \mathrm{kN} / \mathrm{m}^3$, then the factor of safety against the quick condition will be __________ (round off to two decimal places).
explanation:
To calculate the factor of safety (FOS) against the quick condition for a dam, we need to consider the critical hydraulic gradient $ i_C $ and the exit hydraulic gradient $ i_{\text{exit}} $.

Given:

Head difference ($ \Delta h $): 9 m

Number of equipotential drops ($ N_D $): 10

Length of the field nearest to the toe of the dam ($ L $): 1 m

Saturated unit weight of soil ($ \gamma $): 21 kN/m³

Unit weight of water ($ \gamma_w $): 9.81 kN/m³

Calculations:

Exit Hydraulic Gradient ($ i_{\text{exit}} $):

The exit gradient is calculated using the formula:

$ i_{\text{exit}} = \frac{\Delta h}{L} = \frac{H / N_D}{L} $

Plugging in the values:

$ i_{\text{exit}} = \frac{9 / 10}{1} = 0.9 $

Critical Hydraulic Gradient ($ i_C $):

The critical gradient is calculated as:

$ i_C = \frac{\gamma' }{\gamma_w} $

Here, $\gamma' = \gamma - \gamma_w$, therefore:

$ i_C = \frac{21 - 9.81}{9.81} = 1.14 $

Factor of Safety (FOS):

The factor of safety against the quick condition can be calculated as:

$ \mathrm{FOS} = \frac{i_C}{i_{\text{exit}}} = \frac{1.14}{0.9} = 1.27 $

Thus, the factor of safety against the quick condition is 1.27 (rounded off to two decimal places).

content: Let $$\psi $$ represent soil suction head and K represent hydraulic conductivity of the soil. If the soil moisture content $$\theta$$ increases, which one of the following statements is TRUE?
explanation:
$${h_c} \propto {1 \over R}$$

$$K \propto S$$

Water content $$\uparrow$$ $$\to$$ R$$\uparrow$$ R$$\uparrow$$ $$\to$$ hc $$\downarrow$$ $$\to$$ $$\psi$$ $$\downarrow$$

Water content $$\uparrow$$ $$\to$$ S$$\uparrow$$ $$\to$$ K$$\uparrow$$

(a) $$\to$$ $$\psi$$ decreases and K increases.

content: Two soils of permeabilities $\mathrm{k}_1$ and $\mathrm{k}_2$ are placed in a horizontal flow apparatus, as shown in the figure. For Soil 1, $L_1=50 \mathrm{~cm}$, and $k_1=0.055 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$; for Soil 2, $L_2=30 \mathrm{~cm}$, and $\mathrm{k}_2=0.035 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s}$. The cross sectional area of the horizontal pipe is $100 \mathrm{~cm}^2$, and the head difference ( $\Delta \mathrm{h}$ ) is 150 cm . The discharge (in $\mathrm{cm}^3 / \mathrm{s}$ ) through the soils is ________ (rounded off to 2 decimal places).
explanation: $$ \begin{aligned} &\text { For series arrangement of soil } 1 \text { and soil } 2 \text {, }\\ &\text { } \begin{aligned}Discharge,\,\, q & =k_{\text {eq. }} \text { i. } A \text {. } \\ & =\frac{\Sigma z_i}{\Sigma \frac{z_i}{k_i}} \times\left(\frac{H_L}{\text { Length }}\right) \times A \\ & =\frac{50+30}{\frac{50}{0.055}+\frac{30}{0.035}} \times \frac{150}{80} \times 100=8.49 \mathrm{~cm}^3 / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned} \end{aligned} $$
